!!! note "Draft converted resource"
    This page was generated from a Ministry of Health PDF using the document digitization pipeline. Content, tables, figures, and spacing will be reviewed progressively before final publication.

<!-- Page 1 -->

![image 1](data:image/png;base64,)

MINISTRY OF HEALTH

# GOAL ORIENTED ANTENATAL CARE PROTOCOL

Important: Goals are different depending on the timing of the visit. Minimum 8 contacts are aimed for in an Uncomplicated pregnancy. If a woman books later than in first trimester, preceding goals should be combined and attended to. At all visits address any identified problems, check the BP and measure the Symphysio-Fundal Height (SFH)

| |TRIMESTER|GOAL|TIMING OF CONTACT|HISTORY TAKING|EXAMINATION|LABORATORY Investigations|PROMOTION|ACTION|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|FIRSTCONTACT|First Trimester 0 – 12 weeks|-Confirm pregnancy<br>-General/Risk Assessment<br>-Health Education<br>-Plan for delivery<br>-Appropriate preventive interventions<br>-Involve the male partner spouse<br>|Contact 1: Anytime ≤ 12 weeks|-Presenting complaint<br>-LNMP<br>-Estimate period of gestation<br>-Contraceptive?<br>-Obstetric<br>-Medical<br>-Surgical<br>-STI<br>-Social: smoking alcohol/drugs<br>-TB screening<br>-Intimate Partner Violence (IPV)<br>-Dietary<br>|-General exam<br>-Vital exam (e.g. BP, pulse)<br>-SFH measurement<br>-Abdominal/specific exam<br>-Vulva exam (Speculum if indicated)<br>-Nutritional assessment (height, weight, MUAC)<br>|-Hb (CBC where available)<br>-HIV test<br>-Syphilis test (RPR)<br>-Blood group/RhD<br>-Urine albumen, Glucose<br>-Gram staining for ASB, urine culture if indicated<br>-Glucose tolerance test (GTT) (for suspicious cases/hospital)<br>-RDT for Malaria (where indicated)<br>-Hepatitis B test<br>|-H/E on common pregnancy complaints<br>-Address any problem<br>-Involve husband in ANC<br>-Draw up a birth and emergency preparedness plan<br>-Counsel on PPFP methods<br>-Danger Signs (abdominal pain, severe headache, blurred vision etc)<br>-eMTCT<br>-Nutrition education, Hygiene, Rest and exercise<br>-Infant feeding<br>-LLINS, IPTp use<br>-Dangers of smoking, alcohol and substance abuse<br>|-Tetanus/Diphtheria vaccine (Td)<br>- Ferrous SO4<br>-Folic acid<br>-Treat incidental ailments<br>-Condom use for HIV prevention in discordant couples and those at high risk<br>- Debriefing mother on findings and course of action<br>-Give next appointment and explain what will be done emphasising need to come back any time if there is need<br>|
|ndrdCONTACTand32|Second Trimester >13 – 28 weeks|-Respond to abnormal Lab results<br>-Provide preventive measures (Td, IPTp)<br>-Exclude multiple pregnancy and fetal abnormalities<br>-Promote nutrition and wellbeing<br>-Assess for danger signs of Pregnancy Induced Hypertension and any other danger signs<br>-Rule out anaemia<br>|Contact 2: 13 – 20 Weeks<br>Contact 3: 21 – 28 Weeks<br>|- Ask for presenting complaints<br>-Date of 1st foetal movements<br>-vaginal bleeding<br>-Social: smoking alcohol/drugs<br>-TB screening<br>-Intimate partner violence<br>|-General exam<br>-BP<br>-SFH (symphysis Fundal Height)<br>-Abdominal exam<br>-rule out multiple<br>-pregnancy<br>-Nutritional assessment<br>-Early Ultra Sound Scan best at 20 weeks but can be done up to 24 weeks<br>|-Hb at 26 weeks<br>-If BP ≥140/90<br>-Urine albumen, if there is glycosuria refer to hospital for GTT<br>|-Address presenting complaints<br>-Discuss Laboratory results and need to treat partner where necessary<br>-Symptoms of PIH, vaginal bleeding<br>-eMTCT/HCT<br>-LLINs/IPTp use<br>-Danger Signs<br>-Nutrition & Hygiene, Rest and exercise<br>-Male involvement<br>-Birth and emergency preparedness plan<br>|-Td<br>-Ferrous SO4<br>-Folic acid<br>-IPT dose<br>-Mebendazole<br>-Treat incidental ailments<br>-Use of condoms in high risk individuals/discordant<br>-Debriefing mother<br>-Give next appointment and explain what will be done emphasising need to come back any time if there is need<br>|


<!-- Page 2 -->

| |TRIMESTER|GOAL|TIMING OF CONTACT|HISTORY TAKING|EXAMINATION|LABORATORY Investigations|PROMOTION|ACTION|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|ththththand8thCONTACT,6,547|Third Trimester 29 – 40 weeks|-Check foetal growth<br>-Exclude anaemia<br>-Assess for signs of PIH<br>-Review birth and emergency preparedness plan<br>-Exclude abnormal presentation/lie<br>-Review delivery plan<br>|Contact 4 30 weeks<br>Contact 5 34 weeks<br>Contact 6 36 weeks<br>Contact 7 38 weeks<br>Contact 8 40 weeks<br>|-Ask for problems/ complications<br>-Vaginal bleeding<br>-Fetal movements<br>-Intimate partner violence<br>|-General exam<br>-Rule out anaemia<br>-Nutritional assessment<br>-BP<br>-Abdominal exam<br>-Obstetric (SFH)<br>-Check lie presentation<br>|-If BP ≥140/90<br>-Urine albumen<br>-Hb at 36 WOA<br>-Midstream gram staining to rule out Asymptomatic Bacteruria at 34 weeks<br>-Repeat HIV testing and Viral as per current guidelines (36 weeks)<br>|-Address problems<br>-Discuss signs of labour/ PROM<br>-Discuss vaginal bleeding<br>-Review delivery plan<br>-eMTCT/HTS<br>-LLIN/IPTp use<br>-Postpartum FP<br>-Sex and other postpartum Care<br>-Infant Feeding<br>-Danger signs<br>-Nutrition & Hygiene, Rest and exercise<br>-Male involvement<br>-Cervical cancer screening<br>|-Ferrous SO4<br>-Folic acid<br>-IPT dose<br>-Treat incidental ailments<br>-Treat presenting ailments based on lab findings<br>-Use of condoms in high risk individuals/discordant<br>-Debriefing mother<br>-Review and modify birth and emergency preparedness plan<br>|
|Note: If not delivered by 41 weeks, immediately report to the nearest health facility| | | | | | | | |
